title: Nauclea philippinensis
taxonomy: Nauclea philippinensis is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This classification reflects the plant's close relationship with other species within this family. Nauclea philippinensis is further classified as a species within the genus Nauclea. The genus Nauclea is part of the order Gentianales, which consists of a diverse group of flowering plants. The family and order classifications help to establish Nauclea philippinensis within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
uses: Nauclea philippinensis is a plant species that has been utilized in traditional medicine. Its leaves and bark are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. Additionally, it is used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Nauclea philippinensis in treating these conditions.
benefits: Nauclea philippinensis has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, and it is said to have several health benefits. The plant is believed to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. Overall, the plant is considered a valuable resource for its potential health benefits and medicinal properties.

parts: Nauclea philippinensis. side_effects: Nauclea philippinensis, a plant traditionally used in folk medicine, has been reported to cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and vomiting due to its high concentration of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds.
